title: Module 1
content:
Basic concepts: Alphabet, Strings, Languages, Grammar, Automata
Deterministic Finite Automata/Accepter (DFA), Language of a DFA,
Regular languages, Nondeterministic Finite Automata/Accepter (NFA), NFA
with epsilon transitions, Language of an NFA, eliminating epsilon transitions
(Proof not expected), Equivalence of NFAs and DFAs (Proof not expected) -
The Subset Construction. DFA State Minimization, Applications of finite
automata.

title: Module 2
content:
Regular expression, Language of a Regular Expression, Equivalence with
finite automata (Proof not expected) - Converting FA to Regular
Expressions, Converting Regular Expressions to FA, Regular grammar –
examples.
Closure Properties of Regular Languages (Proof not expected), Pumping
Lemma for Regular Languages (Proof not expected), Pumping Lemma as a
tool to prove nonregularity of languages.
Context Free Language (CFL) and Context-Free Grammar (CFG),
Designing CFGs for CFLs, Leftmost and Rightmost Derivations, Parse
Trees, Ambiguity in CFGs and CFLs

title: Module 3
content:
Simplification of Context-Free Grammars - Eliminating useless symbols and
productions, Eliminating epsilon productions, Eliminating unit productions,
Chomsky Normal Form (CNF), Converting CFGs into CNF, Greibach
Normal Form (definition only).
Pushdown Automata (PDA), Language of a PDA, DPDA and NPDA,
Designing PDAs for CFLs. Equivalence NPDAs and CFGs (Proof not
expected)

title: Module 4
content:
Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages (Proof not expected),
Pumping Lemma as a tool to identify Non-Context-Free Languages. Closure
Properties of CFLs (Proof not expected), Context Sensitive Grammar and
Languages (introduction only)
Turing Machines (TMs) - Turing Machines as language acceptors, Language
of a TM, Turing Machines as transducers, Nondeterministic Turing
Machines (definition only), Recursive and Recursively Enumerable
languages, Unrestricted Grammar (definition only), Chomsky hierarchy,
Linear Bounded Automaton as a restricted TM (introduction only).
